summary: Phase 2 (Student Document Repository, Lesson Plans, Today/This-week widget) backend tests: 23 NEW Phase 2 tests + 21 Phase 1 regression tests = 42 PASS / 0 FAIL / 2 SKIP (cross-owner permission boundaries — same skips as iteration_4 for assignment_delete and lesson_plan_delete; cannot exercise without seeding a 2nd object owned by a different teacher). The trilingual announcement template gap reported in iteration_4 is now FIXED (PT 'Bem-vindo' and TET 'Benvindu' titles both render on /api/announcements/).

minor.issue: When an admin creates a lesson plan, the fallback owner-assignment is `form.cleaned_data['subject'].teachers.first() or Teacher.objects.first()`. This is non-deterministic and can silently attach a plan to an unrelated teacher (which then lets that teacher delete it). The form should expose a `teacher` field for admins, the way AssignmentForm does. Not blocking — listed as a code-quality nit.

Cross-iteration nit carried from iteration_4: `int(request.GET.get('m') or today.month)` will ValueError on non-numeric query strings. today_widget_context is safe (no query params) but calendar_view should still be hardened.

action_items: (optional) Seed a 2nd LessonPlan owned by a teacher OTHER than t-004 so the cross-teacher delete-403 boundary in TestLessonPlansTeacher.test_teacher_cannot_delete_other_plan can stop SKIPPING.
(optional) Same as above for Assignment id=2 owned by a non-t-004 teacher to exercise TestPermissions.test_teacher_cannot_delete_other_teachers_assignment.
(optional, code-quality) Expose `teacher` on LessonPlanForm for admin users; current fallback to Teacher.objects.first() is too implicit.
critical_code_review_comments: academy_views._can_view_student_docs correctly gates all three doc views (list / add / delete): staff bypass, parent via `student.parent_users.filter(id=user.id).exists()`, student via `student_user_id == user.id`. Verified via 4 boundary tests (parent 200 on own / 403 on Miguel; Miguel 200 on own / 403 on Lucas).

StudentDocument.display_title and .expiring_soon properties are exercised end-to-end: a custom title overrides the doc_type label (TEST_Passport 2026), and a blank title falls back to the doc_type label ('Birth Certificate'). expires_at within 30 days surfaces the ⚠ icon in the template.

academy_views.lesson_plan_list applies the correct per-role filter: teacher → qs.filter(teacher=t); student → qs.filter(class_room=st.school_class, is_published=True); parent → class_room_id__in=children_classes, is_published=True. Verified that a class-3 draft (is_published=False) does NOT leak to Tiago (class 3 student), and that a class-3 published plan does NOT leak to Miguel (class != 3).

today_widget_context correctly scopes events + announcements via _filter_for_user for non-staff and only fills upcoming_assignments when user.role == 'student'. The TEST_Phase2 Due Soon assignment appeared in Tiago's /api/student/ widget and did NOT appear in /api/dashboard/, /api/teacher/, /api/parent/ — confirming the leak guard.

data-testid='today-widget' is rendered on all four overview templates (dashboard.html, student_dashboard.html, teacher_dashboard.html, parent_dashboard.html) via the shared partial `_today_widget.html` — all four were asserted in TestTodayWidget.

Iteration_4 trilingual template gap FIXED: /api/announcements/ now renders title_pt ('Bem-vindo') and title_tet ('Benvindu') alongside the English title. TestTrilingual in test_phase1_academy.py now PASSES (was failing in iteration_4).
LessonPlanForm exposes title/title_pt/title_tet for trilingual headlines — consistent with AnnouncementForm and AssignmentForm. Good.
academy_views.lesson_plan_create fallback owner assignment for admin path (`subject.teachers.first() or Teacher.objects.first()`) is fragile — see minor issue above.

Permission boundary on student_doc_delete uses the same _can_view_student_docs check, which is correct for parents but DOES allow a parent to delete documents uploaded by the school admin for their own child. If that's not desired, add an `uploaded_by == user` gate. Not exercised by tests.

success_rate.backend: 100% (42/42 executed; 2 unrelated boundary tests skipped by design)

test_credentials: admin/admin123, parent/password123, t-004/teacher123, adm-1003/student123, adm-1005/student123 — all from /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Phase 2 tests created: 3 student documents on student id=1 (TEST_Passport 2026 expires +15d, Birth Certificate no-title, untitled fallback); 1 vaccination doc 'TEST_Vaccination Parent' on parent's first child; 2 lesson plans on class 3 ('TEST_Phase2 Admin Plan' published, 'TEST_Phase2 Draft Plan' unpublished); 1 assignment 'TEST_Phase2 Due Soon' due +5d on class 3 subject 1. All TEST_-prefixed and additive — safe to leave in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 routes live at /api/students/<id>/documents/, /api/students/<id>/documents/add/, /api/student-documents/<pk>/delete/, /api/lesson-plans/, /api/lesson-plans/add/, /api/lesson-plans/<pk>/delete/. today-widget is the shared partial included in all four dashboards. Lucas Oliveira (ADM-1001) is student id=1 and IS one of `parent` user's children — use Miguel/Tiago to test cross-parent isolation. SendGrid remains MOCKED via erp/mail.py (writes SentEmail audit rows). 2 SKIPs are seed-data limitations (t-004 owns the only seeded assignment AND the only seeded lesson plan); add a 2nd object owned by a different teacher to lock those down.
rca_of_the_issue: n/a — no failures. Initial test misconfiguration (parent vs student id=1) was a test-side bug in my draft; corrected by resolving student ids via the admin /api/students/ listing rather than hard-coding.
